verification.note: Reported by the T-PRIME authors (Belgiovine et al., 'T-PRIME: Transformer-based Protocol Identification for Machine-learning at the Edge', IEEE INFOCOM 2024, arXiv:2401.04837v2), NOT reproduced or re-run by this repo. The 0.99 is the paper's time-split single-protocol (DS 3.0, 4-class 802.11 b/g/n/ax) accuracy from Fig. 8 ('both LG and SM models achieve an average classification accuracy >= 99% on time-split test data, with SM slightly out-performing LG'). from_paper_UNCERTAIN because of a PROTOCOL / eval-unit mismatch -- per board doctrine an unpublished or differently-constructed split ALONE would NOT downgrade the tier (that caveat is universal for literature rows); the decisive reason here is the metric definition. (a) EVAL-UNIT MISMATCH (decisive) -- (b) SPLIT CONSTRUCTION (context, non-decisive): our canonical split proto-tprime-wifi4-8010-seed42-v1 is a random 80/10/10 (seed 42) over the same Northeastern dataset neu:h989s8519, whereas the paper's 0.99 is a time-split -- same dataset, different split construction, which on its own does not change the tier. the paper's number is PER-TRANSMISSION (per-packet, 'for single transmissions'); our primary accuracy_overall is PER-WINDOW (per 1536-sample slice), and the paper publishes no per-slice single-protocol figure. So this row is a literature reference point only, NOT directly comparable to a per-window harness-run row. Context values from the same paper: 0.97 (abstract, global OTA single-protocol including the harder cross-room scenario-split), 0.967 (real-time in two unseen rooms). The in-repo reproduced number is now on the board as the self_reported `tprime` row (accuracy_overall 0.995 PER-WINDOW on our within-distribution split, corrected model trained with the official recipe lr=2e-4) -- it reaches the paper's >=99% level and confirms this reference. The earlier in-repo 0.7099 came from a re-implementation with several config errors (dim_feedforward, mean-pool vs flatten head, spurious learned positional encoding, wrong tokenisation order), now fixed to match the official genesys-neu/t-prime code.
